question.text: Deepak wants to understand the difference between gross returns and net returns in AIF track record analysis. Net returns are calculated AFTER deducting:

answer.text: Management fee, performance fee, fund expenses, and carried interest
Only taxes applicable to the investor

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Net returns reflect what the investor actually receives after all fund-level deductions including management fees, performance fees/carried interest, and fund operating expenses. **The Trap:** Gross returns look attractive but don't reflect the actual investor experience. Only deducting management fees gives an incomplete picture. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) also distinguishes between gross and net returns but with simpler fee structures (no carry/waterfall). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always present net returns to clients — SEBI guidelines emphasize transparency. If a fund only provides gross returns, it may be hiding an expensive fee structure.
